briefdescription.para: When the constraint is fully-applied, the constrained value is saved. @SINCE_1_9.28.
When the constraint is removed, the constrained value is discarded. @SINCE_1_9.28.
Enumeration for the action that will happen when the constraint is removed.

detaileddescription.para: The final value may be "baked" i.e. saved permanently. Alternatively the constrained value may be discarded when the constraint is removed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Apply constraint once only e.g. bake a property and keep. @SINCE_2_4.37.
Apply constraint every frame. @SINCE_2_4.37.
Enumeration for the apply-rate of the constraint. @SINCE_2_4.37.
Initial state, or removed. @SINCE_2_5.7.
Apply() called. @SINCE_2_5.7.
ApplyPost() called. @SINCE_2_5.7.
Apply() called, but some object scene offed. Will be applied if all scene on again @SINCE_2_5.7.
ApplyPost() called, but some object scene offed. Will be post-applied if all scene on again @SINCE_2_5.7.
Object destroyed. Could never be applied again. @SINCE_2_5.7.
Enumeration for the state of the constraint. @SINCE_2_5.7.
BAKE.
Creates an uninitialized Constraint; this can be initialized with Constraint::New().

detaileddescription.para: Calling member functions with an uninitialized Constraint handle is not allowed. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Creates a clone of this constraint for another object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
The handle to the property-owning object this constraint is to be cloned for
The new constraint

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This copy constructor is required for (smart) pointer semantics.
This assignment operator is required for (smart) pointer semantics.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
rhs
A reference to the copied handle
A reference to this

briefdescription.para: Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.22
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Adds a constraint source to the constraint.
Applies this constraint to be computed before transform.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The constraint must be initialized.
The target object must still be alive.
The source inputs should not have been destroyed.
This method cannot be called with ApplyPost at the same time.

briefdescription.para: Applies this constraint to be computed after transform.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.23
The constraint must be initialized.
The target object must still be alive.
The source inputs should not have been destroyed.
This method cannot be called with Apply at the same time.

briefdescription.para: Removes this constraint. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Retrieves the object which this constraint is targeting.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The target object

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the property which this constraint is targeting.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The target property

briefdescription.para: Sets the remove action. Constraint::BAKE will "bake" a value when fully-applied.

detaileddescription.para:
In case of Constraint::DISCARD, the constrained value will be discarded, when the constraint is removed. The default value is Constraint::BAKE. @SINCE_1_0.0
action
The remove-action

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the remove action that will happen when the constraint is removed.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The remove-action

briefdescription.para: Sets the apply-rate of the Constraint.

detaileddescription.para: The default is APPLY_ALWAYS (1), meaning that the Constraint will be processed every frame if the scene graph is changing. It may be desirable to process less frequently. For example, SetApplyRate(3) will process once every 3 frames if the scene graph is changing. If the scene graph is not changing, then the constraint task will not be processed, regardless of this value.
The APPLY_ONCE (0) value means that the Constraint will be processed once only, to bake a property and keep it. Repeatedly calling SetApplyRate(APPLY_ONCE) will cause more bake to be taken.
We can change apply-rate even if already Apply() called.

Constraint will be processed at first frame of this API called. After then,
if APPLY_ONCE, Constarint will be skipped.
if not, Constraint will be skipped (applyRate - 1) frames, and then applied, and so on.

simplesect.para: Unlike SetProperty, APPLY_ONCE is useful if we want to set the property with the same function of constraint, and want to ignore other animation or set property changeness ensurely.
If you are using some input-sensitive constraints (like EqualToConstraint), be careful to use it. If applyRate is not APPLY_ALWAYS, some frames will be skipped. @SINCE_2_4.37

briefdescription.para: Queries the apply-rate of the Constraint. @SINCE_2_4.37.
Sets a tag for the constraint so it can be identified later.

detaileddescription.para:
We can only set tag between [ConstraintTagRanges::CUSTOM_CONSTRAINT_TAG_START ConstraintTagRanges::CUSTOM_CONSTRAINT_TAG_END] or ConstraintTagRanges::DEFAULT_TAG
@SINCE_1_0.0
tag
An integer to identify the constraint

briefdescription.para: Gets the tag.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The tag

briefdescription.para: Gets the state of constraint.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.7
The state of current constraint.

briefdescription.para: Gets the number of constraint sources.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.7
The number of constraint sources

briefdescription.para: Gets the constraint source at the specified index.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.7
index
The index of the constraint source to retrieve
The constraint source at the specified index

memberdef.argsstring: (Handle handle, Property::Index targetIndex, void(*function)(P &, const PropertyInputContainer &))
memberdef.name: New

briefdescription.para: Creates a constraint which targets a property using a function or a static class member.

detaileddescription.para: The expected signature, for a Vector3 type for example, of the function is: voidMyFunction(Vector3&,constPropertyInputContainer&);
Create the constraint with this function as follows:

@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
The handle to the property-owning object
targetIndex
The index of the property to constrain
function
The function to call to set the constrained property value
P
The type of the property to constrain
The new constraint

briefdescription.para: Creates a constraint which targets a property using a functor object.

detaileddescription.para: Create the constraint with this object as follows:

@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
The handle to the property-owning object
targetIndex
The index of the property to constraint
object
The functor object whose functor is called to set the constrained property value
P
The type of the property to constrain
T
The type of the object
The new constraint

memberdef.argsstring: (Handle handle, Property::Index targetIndex, const T &object, void(T::*memberFunction)(P &, const PropertyInputContainer &))
memberdef.name: New

briefdescription.para: Creates a constraint which targets a property using an object method.

detaileddescription.para: Create the constraint with this object as follows:

@SINCE_1_0.0
handle
The handle to the property-owning object
targetIndex
The index of the property to constraint
object
The object whose member function is called to set the constrained property value
memberFunction
The member function to call to set the constrained property value
The new constraint
P
The type of the property to constrain
T
The type of the object

briefdescription.para: Downcasts a handle to Constraint handle.

detaileddescription.para:
If handle points to a Constraint object, the downcast produces valid handle. If not, the returned handle is left uninitialized. @SINCE_1_0.0
baseHandle
BaseHandle to an object
Handle to a Constraint object or an uninitialized handle

briefdescription.para: An abstract base class for Constraints.

detaileddescription.para:
This can be used to constrain a property of an object, after animations have been applied. Constraints are applied in the following order:
Constraints are applied to on-stage actors in a depth-first traversal.
For each actor, the constraints are applied in the same order as the calls to Apply().
Constraints are not applied to off-stage actors.

Create a constraint using one of the New methods depending on the type of callback function used. Try to use a C function unless some data needs to be stored, otherwise functors and class methods are also supported.
A constraint can be applied to an object in the following manner:
